title: Stop Prompting Claude. Start Loop Engineering.
summary: Austin Marchese presents loop engineering (Claude Loops) as a paradigm for AI development that replaces single-turn prompting with iterative structured loops. The method defines five building blocks: trigger, execution skills, goal and verification, output and memory, and emphasizes this as a shift made by practitioners like Boris Cherny and Peter Steinberger.
writeup: This video introduces Loop Engineering, also called Claude Loops, a structured method for AI automation that the channel positions as a replacement for traditional prompting. It breaks the technique into five building blocks: Trigger, Execution Skills, Goal and Verification, Output, and Memory. The central shift is from one-off prompts to iterative cycles, where work runs and repeats rather than executing a single request. The framing connects the approach to Claude Code and to prior practices in prompt engineering.
